Preferred_Parent.Parent_String: multiple dwellings [300005703], dwellings [300005425], residential structures [300257729], single built works by function [300004894], single built works by specific type [300004795], single built works (built environment) [300004790], Single Built Works (hierarchy name) [300004789], Built Environment (hierarchy name) [300264550], Objects Facet [300264092]

Descriptive_Note.Note_Text: Buildings, often multiple-story, containing more than one dwelling unit, most of which are designed for domestic use but sometimes including shops and other nonresidential features. Apartment houses have been in use since ancient times, notable examples having been known in ancient Rome and among ancient Native Americans. The apartment house as known today first appeared in Paris and other large European cities in the 18th century, when tall blocks of apartments for middle-class tenants became common.

Oftmals mehrgeschossiges Gebäude, das mehr als eine Wohneinheit umfasst, von denen die meisten für das Wohnen bestimmt sind, aber manchmal auch Geschäfte und andere gewerbliche Einrichtungen einschließt. Das Apartmenthaus ist seit der Antike in Gebrauch, bemerkenswerte Beispiele sind aus dem alten Rom bekannt und unter den Ureinwohnern Nordamerikas. Das Apartmenthaus, wie wir es heute kennen, tauchte erstmals in Paris und anderen großen europäischen Städten im 18. Jahrhundert auf, als große Wohnblöcke für Mieter der Mittelschicht üblich wurden.
通常具多層樓之建築物，包含一個以上居住單位，主要供家庭居住，但有時也包含商店和其他非居家特質。此公寓式住宅自古代便已存在，於古羅馬人及早期美洲原住民間已有知名範例。現代人所熟知的公寓式住宅最早出現於十八世紀的巴黎及其他歐洲大城市，自此時期以後，供中產階級房客居住之公寓大樓漸趨普遍。

Edifícios, muitas vezes de vários andares, contendo mais de uma unidade habitacional, a maioria das quais projetada para uso doméstico, mas às vezes incluindo lojas e outros elementos não residenciais. Casas de apartamentos estão em uso desde os tempos antigos, exemplos notáveis ​​foram conhecidos na Roma antiga e entre os antigos nativos americanos. O prédio de apartamentos como é conhecido hoje apareceu pela primeira vez em Paris e outras grandes cidades europeias no século 18, quando blocos altos de apartamentos para inquilinos de classe média se tornaram comuns.

Term_Source.Page: illustration, 22, "Apts. & Collective Life in 19th-C NY" E. Crowley p.20-46; illus. throughout; pref. term in text.

2000051245/Plazola Cisneros y Plazola Anguiano, Arquitectura Habitacional (1990)
2000051231/The Oxford- Duden Pictorial Spanish and English Dictionary (1995)
